def dedupe(items):
    """Remove duplicate items while preserving original order."""
    seen = set()
    result = []
    for item in items:
        if item not in seen:
            seen.add(item)
            result.append(item)
    return result
</parameter>
</function>

def is_palindrome(s):
    """Return True if s reads the same forwards and backwards.

    Should be case-insensitive and should ignore whitespace.
    """
    cleaned = ''.join(c.lower() for c in s if c.isalnum())
    return cleaned == cleaned[::-1]

arguments.content:
def is_palindrome(s):
    """Return True if s reads the same forwards and backwards.

    Should be case-insensitive and should ignore whitespace.
    """
    cleaned = ''.join(c.lower() for c in s if c.isalnum())
    return cleaned == cleaned[::-1]

agent_raw_output:
The issue is that when `len(items) % n == 0`, the last chunk is empty. The fix needs to handle this case.

<tool_call>
<function=Edit>
<parameter=file_path>
src/ranges.py
</parameter>
<parameter=content>
def partition(items, n):
    """Split items into n roughly-equal chunks.

    The last chunk should receive any remainder so all items end up somewhere.
    """
    if n <= 0:
        return []
    if n >= len(items):
        return [items[i:i+1] if i < len(items) else [] for i in range(n)]
    
    k = len(items) // n
    remainder = len(items) % n
    result = []
    start = 0
    for i in range(n):
        chunk_size = k + (1 if i < remainder else 0)
        result.append(items[start:start + chunk_size])
        start += chunk_size
    return result
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

arguments.content:
def partition(items, n):
    """Split items into n roughly-equal chunks.

    The last chunk should receive any remainder so all items end up somewhere.
    """
    if n <= 0:
        return []
    if n >= len(items):
        return [items[i:i+1] if i < len(items) else [] for i in range(n)]
    
    k = len(items) // n
    remainder = len(items) % n
    result = []
    start = 0
    for i in range(n):
        chunk_size = k + (1 if i < remainder else 0)
        result.append(items[start:start + chunk_size])
        start += chunk_size
    return result
